title: bot/scripting.rs: hook child not killed on timeout
description:
bot/src/scripting.rs ~line 64: the timeout branch drops the child future without calling child.kill() first. The spawned subprocess continues running indefinitely. Under a slow or hung hook script, zombie processes accumulate for the entire bot process lifetime with no bound.

Root cause: tokio::time::timeout drops the future on expiry; the Child handle is not signalled.

Fix: in the timeout Err branch, call child.kill().await before returning the error, then child.wait().await to reap the process table entry.
